mappings.rationale: Directly addresses the molecular level of biotic organization, including polymers, carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids that define the properties of life.
Covers the cellular level of biotic organization, including procaryotic and eucaryotic cells, which is fundamental to understanding characteristics of living things.
Addresses the molecular basis of metabolism (catabolism and anabolism) and the chemical properties underlying life processes.
Covers molecular-level organization of life including nucleic acids, proteins, and the molecular machinery of reproduction and development.
Directly addresses the concept of extraterrestrial life and its chemistry, and the significance of the search for life beyond Earth.
Illustrates the population and community levels of biotic organization, showing how microbial communities interact with their environment.
Covers cellular-level life including procaryotic organisms, their metabolism, reproduction, and interaction with the environment.
Addresses the integration of multiple levels of biotic organization from molecular to organ to population, a central theme of characterizing living things.
Explores the fundamental properties of life by examining how living systems can be engineered, illuminating order, metabolism, and reproduction.
Covers the chemistry of carbon-based molecules including carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids that form the molecular basis of life.
